hints.text: Use a binary heap (heapq) for the open set to efficiently get the node with minimum f-score. Store tuples as (f_score, counter, node) where counter breaks ties to avoid comparing nodes directly
Cache heuristic values in a dictionary to avoid recomputation. For better performance, consider using a closed set implemented as a set() rather than checking if nodes exist in came_from

For dynamic replanning, store the previous path and only recompute when obstacles appear along the current path. Use lazy evaluation: don't immediately replan when the map updates, wait until the next planning request
